info.title: Beyond Queue
info.description: PostgreSQL-native message queue with SQS-compatible and REST APIs.

get.summary:
List all subscriptions for an event pattern. Returns subscriptions whose pattern
exactly matches the given value.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Exact event pattern to look up.

post.summary:
Subscribe a queue or HTTP endpoint to an event pattern. Messages published to any
routing key that matches `pattern` (glob syntax) will be delivered to this subscriber.
post.description:
Queue subscription: provide `queue_name` — messages are enqueued directly.
Webhook subscription: provide `protocol` (`"http"` or `"https"`) and `endpoint` URL.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Glob pattern matched against routing keys at publish time (e.g. `payments.*`, `**` to match all events).

delete.summary:
Remove an event subscription by ID. Idempotent: returns 404 if the subscription
no longer exists.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Event pattern (for URL structure; the subscription ID alone uniquely identifies the record).

post.summary:
Publish a message to an event routing key. The routing key is matched against all
subscription patterns (glob syntax). One copy of the message is enqueued in each
matching queue and an HTTP POST is dispatched to each matching HTTP/HTTPS endpoint.
post.description:
HTTP subscribers receive the raw message body or an SNS-style notification envelope
depending on how the subscription was created (`envelope` flag at subscribe time).

parameters.name: routing_key
parameters.description: Routing key matched against subscription patterns using glob syntax (e.g. `payments.created`, `orders.*`).

201.description: Message published. Returns the matched queue count and per-queue message IDs.

404.description: No subscriptions match the routing key.

get.summary: List all queues. Returns metadata for every queue in the system, ordered by name.

post.summary:
Create a new queue. Standard queues offer at-least-once, unordered delivery. FIFO
queues (`fifo: true`) guarantee per-group ordering at lower throughput. Returns 201
on success; the queue name is reserved immediately.

get.summary: Fetch real-time metrics for a single queue: depth, message age, and total throughput.

delete.summary:
Delete a queue and all of its messages permanently. Idempotent: safe to call even
if the queue is already gone.

get.summary:
Receive up to `max` messages from a queue. Received messages are hidden from other
consumers for `vt` seconds (visibility timeout). Delete them after processing to
prevent re-delivery. If `wait` > 0 and the queue is empty, the call long-polls for
up to that many seconds before returning an empty array. Set `fifo=true` to receive
messages in strict enqueue order within each `group_id`.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return. Default: 1.

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description:
Long-poll wait time in seconds. The call blocks until a message arrives or the
timeout elapses. Default: 0 (no wait — returns immediately).

parameters.name: vt
parameters.description:
Visibility timeout in seconds. Received messages are hidden from other consumers
for this duration. Delete the message before it elapses to prevent re-delivery.
Default: 30.

parameters.name: fifo
parameters.description:
Return messages in FIFO order within each `group_id`. Only applies to FIFO
queues. Default: false.

200.description: Up to `max` messages. Empty array when the queue is empty or the long-poll wait expires.

post.summary:
Send one or more messages to a queue. The body is either a single message object or
a JSON array of message objects — the shape is detected automatically.
post.description:
Batch sends with a uniform `delay` value are committed in a single statement for
maximum throughput. Set `async_commit=true` to skip WAL fsync for best-effort,
high-speed sends at the cost of crash durability.

parameters.name: async_commit
parameters.description:
Skip WAL fsync on commit. Improves throughput at the cost of durability:
messages can be lost on a PostgreSQL crash. Default: false (durable).

201.description: Message(s) enqueued. Single send returns `{"id": <n>}`; batch send returns `{"ids": [...]}`.

delete.summary:
Acknowledge and delete multiple messages in one request. Non-existent IDs are
silently ignored. The response lists only the IDs that were actually deleted.

200.description: IDs of messages that were deleted.

delete.summary:
Acknowledge and permanently delete a single message. Safe to call multiple times —
returns 404 if the message has already been deleted or never existed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Message ID returned by send or receive.

patch.summary:
Extend or reduce the visibility timeout of an in-flight message. Use this to
prevent re-delivery when processing takes longer than the original `vt`, or to
make a message immediately visible again by setting `vt=0`.

post.summary:
Delete all messages in a queue without removing the queue itself. Returns the count
of messages removed.

get.summary: List all topic subscriptions targeting this queue.

200.description: Active topic subscriptions for the queue.

ChangeVisibilityRequest.description: Request body to extend or reduce a message's visibility timeout.

vt.description:
New visibility timeout in seconds from now. The message will not be returned by
receive calls until this duration elapses. Set to `0` to make the message
immediately visible.

ChangeVisibilityResponse.description: Confirmation of a visibility timeout change.

visible_at.description: Updated timestamp after which the message becomes visible again.

fifo.description:
When `true`, creates a FIFO queue: messages with the same `group_id` are delivered
in strict enqueue order. Default: `false` (standard queue, at-least-once unordered).

name.description:
Queue name. Must be unique within the instance. Letters, digits, hyphens, and
underscores only.

DeleteBatchRequest.description: Request body for batch message deletion.

ids.description: IDs of messages to delete. Non-existent IDs are silently skipped.

DeletedResponse.description: Result of a batch delete operation.

deleted.description: IDs of the messages that were actually deleted.

ErrorBody.description: Inner error payload for all non-2xx responses.

hint.description: Optional actionable guidance present on configuration-gate errors.

ErrorResponse.description: Wire-format error envelope returned on all non-2xx responses.

enqueued_at.description: Timestamp when the message was added to the queue.

headers.description: Metadata attached at send time. `null` when none was provided.

id.description: Message ID. Use this to delete or extend the visibility of the message.

read_count.description:
How many times this message has been delivered. Starts at `1` on first delivery.
A value greater than `1` indicates a prior consumer did not delete the message
before its visibility timeout expired.

visible_at.description:
Timestamp after which the message becomes visible again if not deleted. Extends
on each `PATCH /v1/queues/{name}/messages/{id}` call.

deleted.description: Number of messages deleted from the queue.

newest_msg_age_sec.description: Age in seconds of the newest visible message. `null` when the queue is empty.

oldest_msg_age_sec.description: Age in seconds of the oldest visible message. `null` when the queue is empty.

queue_length.description: Number of messages currently visible (not hidden by a visibility timeout).

scrape_time.description: Timestamp when these metrics were sampled.

total_messages.description: Total messages ever enqueued, including already-deleted ones.

QueueResponse.description: Queue metadata returned by list operations.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the queue was created.

is_partitioned.description: `true` when the queue is backed by a partitioned table for high-throughput workloads.

is_unlogged.description:
`true` when the queue uses an unlogged table — writes are faster but messages are
not crash-safe.

oneOf.title: BatchSend
oneOf.description: Send multiple messages in one request.

SendBody.description:
Request body for send — either a single message object or a JSON array for batch
sends. The shape is detected automatically: a JSON object is a single send, a JSON
array is a batch.

delay.description:
Delivery delay in seconds. The message becomes visible to receivers after this
many seconds. Default: `0` (immediately visible).

group_id.description:
FIFO group identifier. Messages with the same `group_id` are delivered in strict
enqueue order. Requires a FIFO queue. All messages in a batch must include a
`group_id` if any do.

headers.description:
Optional key-value metadata to attach to the message. Any JSON object. Delivered
alongside the body on receive.

message.description: Message body. Any JSON value: object, array, string, number, or boolean.

ids.description: Assigned message IDs, in the same order as the request array.

SubscribeRequest.description:
Request body to create an event subscription. Provide either `queue_name` (to route
into an internal queue) or `protocol` + `endpoint` (for HTTP/HTTPS webhook delivery).
The two forms are mutually exclusive.

endpoint.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL to POST the message to. Required when `protocol` is set.

envelope.description:
When `true`, HTTP/HTTPS subscribers receive an SNS-compatible notification
envelope instead of the raw payload. Default: `false` (raw delivery).

protocol.description:
Delivery protocol for webhook subscriptions. One of `"http"` or `"https"`.
Required when `queue_name` is absent. Mutually exclusive with `queue_name`.

queue_name.description:
Name of an existing queue to deliver matching messages to. Mutually exclusive
with `protocol` and `endpoint`.

TopicMessage.description: A message enqueued in a single matched queue as a result of a topic publish.

msg_id.description: Assigned message ID within that queue.

queue_name.description: Name of the queue that received the message.

TopicSendRequest.description: Request body for publishing a message to an event routing key.

delay.description: Delivery delay in seconds applied to each enqueued message. Default: `0`.

headers.description: Optional metadata to attach to each enqueued copy. Any JSON object.

message.description: Message body to fan out to all matching queues and HTTP endpoints. Any JSON value.

TopicSendResponse.description: Result of an event publish operation.

messages.description: One entry per matched queue, containing the queue name and assigned message ID.

queues_matched.description: Number of queues that matched the routing key and received a copy of the message.

bound_at.description: Timestamp when the subscription was created.

endpoint.description:
Delivery endpoint. `sqs://<queue_name>` for queue subscriptions; the HTTP/HTTPS URL
for webhook subscriptions.

id.description: Subscription ID. Use this to unsubscribe via `DELETE /v1/events/{pattern}/subscriptions/{id}`.

pattern.description: Glob pattern matched against routing keys at publish time.

protocol.description:
Delivery protocol: `"sqs"` for internal queue delivery, `"http"` or `"https"` for
webhook delivery.

queue_name.description:
Name of the target queue. Present only for `sqs` protocol subscriptions; `null` for
HTTP/HTTPS webhook subscriptions.

raw_delivery.description:
`true` when the message payload is delivered as-is (raw); `false` when wrapped in an
SNS-compatible notification envelope.
